sentence_reviews.text: Paged attention manages key–value cache blocks through virtual-memory-like allocation so variable-length generations share accelerator memory efficiently.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Kwon et al. introduce PagedAttention for LLM serving, using virtual-memory-inspired paging to manage KV-cache blocks and reduce memory waste for variable-length requests.
sentence_reviews.notes: Directly supported by the PagedAttention paper.

sentence_reviews.text: Operational gains introduce new failure modes in concurrency, versioning, security, numerical behavior, observability, and cost. Its direct dependencies include Batching, admission control & GPU scheduling, KV cache & recurrent inference state.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, Paged attention & KV-memory management is modeled as depending on Batching, admission control & GPU scheduling, KV cache & recurrent inference state and feeding downstream nodes such as Large language models, Conversational assistants & chatbots.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
